topic: interpreting data sets, counting data values, identifying units
latex:
You keep track of your test scores one semester. Your results are:

$93, 87, 92, 88, 90$

\textbf{Part A} Find and interpret the number of data values.

There are \underline{\hspace{1cm}} data values. So, you had \underline{\hspace{1cm}} tests in one semester.

\textbf{Part B} What are the units?

\begin{enumerate}
\item[a.] $\bigcirc$ inches
\item[b.] $\bigcirc$ Richter magnitudes
\item[c.] $\bigcirc$ percents
\item[d.] $\bigcirc$ liters
\end{enumerate}
answer_latex: Part A: 5 data values, 5 tests; Part B: c. percents
